title: Evangelions 30th Anniversary
wikitext:
[]: Evangelion's 30th Anniversary             Quote Post by FelipeFritschF » Fri Oct 04, 2024 2:05 am The official Eva Twitter account just teased that "lots of fun things" is coming for Eva's 30th anniversary... Machine translation wrote: Today, October 4th, is the anniversary of the broadcast of the first episode of #NeonGenesisEvangelion. This year marks the 29th year since its creation in 1995. Next year marks the 30th anniversary, so we have lots of fun things planned for you. Please keep an eye out! We hope you will continue to support the #Evangelion series.

[]: Re: Evangelion's 30th Anniversary             Quote Post by The Killer of Heroes » Fri Oct 04, 2024 9:21 am Maybe they’ll announce a remake…a sort of “Rebuild” of Evangelion if you will.

[]: Re: Evangelion's 30th Anniversary             Quote Post by Axx°N N. » Fri Oct 04, 2024 1:41 pm Perhaps they've been putting off the Q & Shin companion books to tie in with the hype?

[]: Re: Evangelion's 30th Anniversary             Quote Post by BernardoCairo » Fri Oct 04, 2024 3:39 pm I'm just happy we're getting something. It can be anything really. I'm happy we will have the celebration Felipe has been talking about for years now The Killer of Heroes wrote: Maybe they’ll announce a remake…a sort of “Rebuild” of Evangelion if you will.

[]: Re: Evangelion's 30th Anniversary             Quote Post by Voyager » Sun Oct 06, 2024 12:32 am Here's hoping.

[]: Re: Evangelion's 30th Anniversary             Quote Post by Dominicmgm » Fri Nov 08, 2024 3:01 pm One thing I am hoping for is that All the Anime does another rerelease of The End of Evangelion in the UK next year. They did it in 2021, but I didn't go as I hadn't seen the show at the time. Given that GKIDS' US rerelease was a big deal, I can see it being on the cards.

[]: Re: Evangelion's 30th Anniversary             Quote Post by Javi2541997 » Fri Dec 20, 2024 8:25 am I guess they would be up to releasing some collectors stuff; I imagine some sort of memorial figure and so on. First, in Japan; then, the USA and Canada; and finally, in Europe.

[]: Re: Evangelion's 30th Anniversary             Quote Post by FelipeFritschF » Tue Dec 31, 2024 11:19 am tweet-body — twitter-user (@username) date document.querySelectorAll('blockquote.twitter-tweet > a').forEach((t) => t.href = t.href.replace('x.com', 'twitter.com')) https://30th.evangelion.jp/ Three day event announced for Feb 21-23... 2026 !

[]: Re: Evangelion's 30th Anniversary             Quote Post by Joseki » Tue Dec 31, 2024 5:06 pm My very early prediction is that on the last day of this event they will announce the next Evangelion project and it's a series/movie NOT directed/written by Anno. Clean reboot.

[]: Re: Evangelion's 30th Anniversary             Quote Post by Axx°N N. » Tue Dec 31, 2024 7:07 pm Joseki wrote: My very early prediction is that on the last day of this event they will announce the next Evangelion project and it's a series/movie NOT directed/written by Anno. Clean reboot.

[]: Re: Evangelion's 30th Anniversary             Quote Post by BernardoCairo » Wed Jan 01, 2025 5:35 pm I think it's possible that we will see smaller projects this year and then an actual Evangelion remake next year. A visual update for the original series is something I can see as being possible. It would be easy to do without Anno and it would also be a good fit for the 30th anniversary brand.

[]: Re: Evangelion's 30th Anniversary             Quote Post by DTGee » Wed Jan 01, 2025 7:03 pm Well, Gundam GQX is coming out in the begining of the year, and then Khara will start working on the Space Battleship Yamato movie, so February 2026 does seem to be when they'd be ready to announce what their next project will be if it is Eva.

[]: Re: Evangelion's 30th Anniversary             Quote Post by Joseki » Thu Jan 02, 2025 7:39 am Axx°N N. wrote: Can't believe I'm reading into a semicolon, but I'd say the event title bears out your prediction; related but separate.

[]: Re: Evangelion's 30th Anniversary             Quote Post by FelipeFritschF » Thu Jan 02, 2025 9:56 am Axx°N N.#944229 wrote: Can't believe I'm reading into a semicolon, but I'd say the event title bears out your prediction; related but separate.

[]: Re: Evangelion's 30th Anniversary             Quote Post by cyharding » Thu Jan 02, 2025 12:15 pm BernardoCairo wrote: I think it's possible that we will see smaller projects this year and then an actual Evangelion remake next year.

[]: Re: Evangelion's 30th Anniversary             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu Jan 02, 2025 12:28 pm FelipeFritschF wrote: (The forum doesn't support the actual repetition sign character...)

[]: Re: Evangelion's 30th Anniversary             Quote Post by Jinroh » Fri Jan 03, 2025 10:01 am I don't want to be excited for nothing, but still I hope they'll announce some major projects. Anyway it's funny the logo is that of an impact "gate", and it has the colors of the last 3 movies released on bluray in Japan; orange/blue/white.

[]: Re: Evangelion's 30th Anniversary             Quote Post by Joseki » Sun Jan 05, 2025 7:44 am The colors are the ones associated to every Eva entry: Black (NGE) Red (1.0) Orange (2.0) Blu (3.0) White (3.0+1.0) https://www.evangelion.jp/

[]: Re: Evangelion's 30th Anniversary             Quote Post by Ringworm128 » Sun Jan 05, 2025 8:41 pm My unrealistic wish is them announcing a proper big budget Eva video game. Not another VN or cheap cash in.

[]: Re: Evangelion's 30th Anniversary             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Mon Jan 06, 2025 7:15 am IF there's a new Eva work, it'll be announced there. And if it happens I hope it'd completely different from prior stuff.
